ISS608 Project · Group 1

Forecasting
Singapore
Customer Price Index (CPI)

RShiny App About the Project
6
Years of CPI data (2020–2025)
4
Forecasting models compared
51
CPI leaf-node series forecasted
1.69
Lowest avg RMSE (ETS model)
About

Understanding Singapore's CPI

Singapore's CPI is a critical barometer for policymakers, businesses, and households. As a highly open economy, Singapore's price levels are influenced by global commodity cycles, exchange rate movements, domestic wage growth, and housing costs.

This project develops and evaluates a suite of time-series and machine-learning forecasting models to anticipate CPI movements across headline, core, and sub-component indices — enabling sharper economic planning and policy analysis.

Data is sourced from CEIC Data.

01 · Objective

Evaluate and compare time-series forecasting models to generate 12-month ahead CPI forecasts for Singapore across all expenditure categories.

02 · Methodology

ETS, Auto-ARIMA, ARIMA+XGBoost, and Prophet models, evaluated using nested forecasting with a 12-month holdout test set. Forecasts are reconciled bottom-up using official DOS basket weights.

03 · Data Sources

CEIC Database via SMU Library. Monthly Singapore CPI data (Base: 2024 = 100), covering January 2020 to December 2025.

04 · Tools

Built in R with modeltime, tidymodels, timetk, and plotly. Interactive dashboard developed in R Shiny. Documented via Quarto.

05 · Audience

Economists, policy researchers, and data scientists interested in Singapore macroeconomics and inflation dynamics.

RShiny App

Interactive Modules

Explore Singapore's CPI data through three interactive modules built in R Shiny — from raw data exploration to model-based forecasting.

MODULE · 01
↗
Exploratory Data Analysis
Explore Singapore CPI trends, seasonal patterns, and temporal dependencies across expenditure categories. Includes time series plots, seasonal diagnostics, and ACF/PACF analysis.
MODULE · 02
↗
Time Series Decomposition
Decompose CPI series into trend, seasonal, and remainder components using STL. Examine the relative dominance of each component across CPI categories.
MODULE · 03
↗
Forecasting
Explore 12-month CPI forecasts generated using ETS, ARIMA, ARIMA+XGBoost, and Prophet. Switch between auto mode (best model by RMSE) and manual model selection across all CPI hierarchy levels.
SG CPI Forecast
Built with R Quarto | CEIC Data
